free_text: Isotopic neon cross sections for a study of neutron
Prelim. MACS at kT=30keV for Ne-20,21,22 in text.
Point-wise tot.CS in 7-800 keV interval on Figures
Neon gas, contained in stainless steel containers,
mounted on an automatic sample changer.
For the Ne-20 measurements were used
The gas pressure was 150 atm. (Transmission exp.)
In case of the capture measurements the sample
Total and capture cross sections were measured in
The flight path was 3 m for the transmitted neutrons
and 60 cm for the capture events.
Spectra for the different isotopes were measured
cyclically in runs of about 10 minutes.
The transmission was higher than 95 per cent.
In order to obtain a large enough signal from
neutron captures in the neon, the experimental
set-up was designed to reduce the background, while
allowing the detectors to be placed at a distance of
2 cm from the sample. Besides the TOF signal the
detector pulse height was also recorded in order
to use the Meyer-Leibnitz pulse height
Li-6 glass detector to monitor neutron flux
C(6)D(6) liquid scin.detectors coupled to fast
Transmitted neutrons were detected by a 10 X 10 cm
B-10 slab, through the 478 keV gamma-rays of the
Capture events were counted by detecting gamma-ray
The total C.S. measurements were needed to correct
the capture data for the background. Thus the
background from scattered neutrons was determined
using the graphite TOF spectrum (for the shape)
and the total C.S. measurements (for the
From the discussion of the S-process neutron balance
it was found that neutron capture in the neon
isotopes is a minor effect thus not effecting the
Lower limit for S-process temperature is given.
Various corrections were made to the capture data.
In the ERR-ANALYSIS the applied corrections are
given together with the related uncertainties.
The negative capture cross section values are due to
the correction for scattered neutron background.
Details of this correction are described in the main

Data units corrected from MB to MICRO-B
MACS were determined using measured point-wise cross
section in energy range 5-200 keV. Contribution of
neutrons of energy > 200 keV was considered as
negligible. Neutrons of the energy range 0-5 keV
represent less than 5% of total neutrons and could
increase the MACS by at most a few percent.
